---
title: "Bahaman Aster (Symphyotrichum bahamense)"
slug: "symphyotrichum-bahamense"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180801703/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Bahaman Aster"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Asterales"
tax_family: "Asteraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Asterales", "Asteraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180801703/medium.jpg" alt="Bahaman Aster" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/betseyboughton">
betseyboughton</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/107492972">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Asterales
-   **Family:** Asteraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=342662)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common in Florida found along roadsides in calcareous soils.

## Natural history notes

A low-growing coastal wildflower characterized by its fleshy leaves and small daisy-like flowers with pale purple to white rays and yellow centers. In Florida, it is found in coastal habitats such as dunes, beach edges, and saline flats. It typically blooms during the cooler months and provides an important nectar source for pollinators in coastal ecosystems.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/342662-Symphyotrichum-bahamense)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/4231)
